steps.title: Square the scale factor
steps.content: Area uses two dimensions, length and width. If every side length is multiplied by $96$, both dimensions in the area calculation are multiplied by $96$, so areas are multiplied by $96^2$.

steps.title: Compute the area factor
steps.content: $$96^2=96\cdot96=9{,}216$$So the area of square A is $9{,}216$ times the area of square B. The answer is D.
